Items.Title: apt update vs apt upgrade：有什么区别？ | apt update vs apt upgrade: What's the difference?
Items.Description:
包管理器太棒了！
让我告诉你是什么让我这么说。

包管理器的特点：

  • 从存储库下载、安装和更新现有软件。
  • 确保包裹的完整性和真实性。
  • 管理和解决所需的依赖关系。
  • 知道将所有文件放在 Linux 文件系统中的什么位置。

想象一下执行校验和验证、安装依赖项、手动将文档、二进制文件和配置文件等组件存储在不同文件夹中的任务。

尽管对于某些包管理器，例如"dpkg" ，您仍然需要手动安装依赖项。 但是我不知道如果我们有更高级的包管理器，它们在后台使用这些低级包管理器并简化任务，为什么还要使用像 dpkg 这样的包管理器。

如果你曾经用谷歌搜索过how to install <package_name> in Ubuntu machine ，你可能会遇到以下命令：

 sudo apt update sudo apt upgrade sudo apt install <package_name>

您可能会认为我们只想安装某个软件包，那么为什么人们建议我们执行这两个附加命令？

让我们明白这一点。

包源列表包含安装包的一些存储库的位置或URL 。

您可以使用以下命令查看此列表：

 cat /etc/apt/sources.list

它会给出输出：

这些存储库中的一个这样的位置是/ubuntu/dists/bionic-updates/main/source （您可以在此处查看），其中Sources.gz文件包含有关软件包的所有信息，包括：

  • 包的依赖
  • 最新版本的软件包
  • 包的校验和以验证完整性
  • 还有更多，如下图所示。

因此，每当我们输入apt update时，它​​都会从存储库中浏览这些列表并将它们的最新版本复制到本地系统。 它实际上并没有在系统上安装任何软件包。

那么 apt upgrade 做什么呢？

通常，在升级系统中的所有软件包时，我们将apt update和apt upgrade命令合并在一行中：

 sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade

所有这些事情都在几秒钟内发生在后端。



原文:

Package Managers are fantastic!
Let me tell you what made me say that.

Features of Package Managers:

  • Downloads, installs and updates existing software from a repository.
  • Ensures the integrity and authenticity of the package.
  • Manages and resolves required dependencies.
  • Knows where to put all the files in the Linux file system.

Just imagine performing tasks like checksum verification, installing dependencies, storing components like docs, binaries and config files in different folders manually.

Although for some package managers like "dpkg", you still need to install dependencies manually. But I don't know why would one use package managers like dpkg if we have more advanced package managers which use these low level package managers under the hood and simplifies the task.

"apt" is one such high level package manager.

If you ever googled how to install <package_name> in Ubuntu machine, you'd have probably came across these commands:

sudo apt update
sudo apt upgrade
sudo apt install <package_name>

You might be thinking that we only wanted to install a certain package so why are people suggesting us to execute those 2 additional commands?

Let's understand that.

What does apt update do?

"apt update" updates the package sources list with the latest versions of the packages in the repositories.

Package sources list contains the locations or URLs of some of the repositories from which a package is installed.

You can view this list using the command :

cat /etc/apt/sources.list

It would give output:

One such location in these repositories is /ubuntu/dists/bionic-updates/main/source (You can check that here) where Sources.gz file contains all the information about the packages including:

  • Dependencies of the package
  • Latest versions of the package
  • Checksums of the packages to verify integrity
  • And much more as shown in the figure below.

So, whenever we type in apt update it browses these lists from the repositories and copies the latest version of them to the local system. It actually doesn't install any package on the system.

What does apt upgrade do then?

"apt upgrade" compares the version of all the packages currently installed on the system with the ones in the list we fetched through apt update and upgrades all of them to their latest versions.

Generally, when upgrading all the packages in the system, we merge both apt update and apt upgrade commands in a single line:

sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade

All these things happen in the backend within a few seconds.
Items.Author: sneh27

Items.Title: Tracy Chou 将在 CodeLand 2022 发表演讲！ | Tracy Chou to Speak at CodeLand 2022!
Items.Description:
在此处了解有关 CodeLand 的更多信息并在此处注册。

我们很高兴与大家分享Tracy Chou将加入@ben （DEV 创始人兼 Forem 联合创始人）参加 CodeLand 2022 的炉边谈话！ 这里有更多关于 Tracy Chou 的信息，可以帮助您对这次富有启发性的对话感到兴奋……

代词：她/她
头衔： Block Party创始人兼CEO
来自：伦敦
简介： Tracy Chou 是一位企业家和软件工程师，她以倡导技术多元化和包容性的工作而闻名。 她目前是 Block Party 的创始人兼首席执行官，该公司为在线安全和反骚扰构建工具。 她还是 Project Include 的联合创始人，这是一个非营利组织，致力于创建一个每个人都有公平机会成功的技术生态系统。 有趣的事实： Tracy 是一个狂热的读者，喜欢按颜色组织她的书。 我们很高兴您能在 CodeLand 2022 上体验 Tracy Chou 和 Ben Halpern 之间的炉边谈话！

在活动、会议和广播领域，“炉边聊天”一词用于描述与有影响力的领导者进行的非正式但结构化的对话，他们的见解和想法许多社区都可以从中受益。

我们认为，我们的早期职业开发者观众将受益于并享受与 Tracy Chou 的炉边谈话——这是我们行业变革和创新的鼓舞人心的力量。

请继续关注 CodeNewbie 社区和 Twitter 上的@codelandconf以获取新闻和更新。 您可以在此处浏览节目并在此处注册活动。 ✨ ✨



原文:

Cross-Posted from CodeNewbie Community.

CodeLand 2022 is a welcoming virtual conference for early-career coders and their champions, taking place on June 16 & 17. We're hard at work building an inspiring, practical, and inclusive program of talks, activities, and more.

CodeLand 2022 is produced by CodeNewbie and DEV, two software communities built on Forem.

Learn more about CodeLand here and register here.

We're thrilled to share that Tracy Chou will be joining @ben (Creator of DEV & Co-Founder of Forem) for a fireside chat at CodeLand 2022! 🎉

Here's a bit more about Tracy Chou to help get you excited for this enlightening conversation...

Pronouns: She/Her
Title: Founder & CEO of Block Party
Based out of: London
Bio: Tracy Chou is an entrepreneur and software engineer known for her work advocating for diversity and inclusion in tech. She is currently the founder and CEO of Block Party, which builds tools for online safety and anti-harassment. She is also a co-founder of Project Include, a non-profit working to create a tech ecosystem where everyone has a fair chance to succeed. In 2013, her Medium article “Where are the numbers?” helped jumpstart the practice of tech companies disclosing their diversity data. Tracy was an early engineer at Pinterest, Quora, and the U.S. Digital Service.
Fun Fact: Tracy is an avid reader and likes to organize her books by color. 🌈

We're so excited for you to experience this fireside chat between Tracy Chou and Ben Halpern at CodeLand 2022!

"What's a 'fireside chat'?"

In the world of events, conferences, and broadcasting, the term "fireside chat" is used to describe an informal yet structured conversation with influential leaders whose insights and thoughts many communities could benefit from accessing.

We think that our audience of early-career developers will benefit from and enjoy this fireside chat with Tracy Chou — an inspiring force of change and innovation in our industry.

"What's next on the road to CodeLand 2022?"

Stay tuned to CodeNewbie Community and @codelandconf on Twitter for news and updates. You can browse the program here and register for the event here. ✨
Items.Author: graciegregory

Items.Title: 制作出色 CLI 应用程序的 14 个重要技巧 | 14 great tips to make amazing CLI applications
Items.Description:
我发现命令行工具是一种令人难以置信的方式来增加它们产生的代码的影响。

您是否编写了一个不错的库来包装 API 并将其连接到您的数据库模型？ 然后提供一个 CLI 工具，允许任何人查询 API、与数据库模型交互、运行您提供的操作、发现所有可用功能并阅读 API 文档。 您现在可以轻松地编写脚本，在开发时执行您的代码的不同功能，创建预提交脚本，创建 CICD 脚本。

以下是我多年来收集的编写 CLI 工具的技巧。

使用命令行解析框架

虽然手动解析argv很有趣，但我直接使用全功能的命令行解析框架，例如 go 中的cobra 、 clap in rust、
在 python 中单击，在 PHP 中单击php-cli 。

这些不仅完善，它们通常允许您使用子命令，为多个 shell 生成自动完成脚本，处理 CLI 选项的漂亮打印并链接到配置框架（见下文）。

将工具作为单个文件分发，没有依赖关系

为确保您的 CLI 工具得到广泛采用，它必须易于安装。 没有人想安装 80 个节点模块或上帝保佑，设置一个 python virtualenv，只是为了使用你的工具。

  • 您还可以尝试在 Common Lisp 中转储世界图像。
  • 另一种选择是创建一个可以通过单个 docker 调用运行的 docker 映像，尽管这通常会使网络和文件 IO 更加复杂。
  • 一个折衷方案是通过你的语言包管理器（pip、npm、你有什么）使它成为一个易于安装的包

使文档成为工具本身的一部分

与其将文档作为单独的 wiki 分发，不如将其作为您工具的一部分。 不要吐出神秘的自动生成的参数列表，而是利用您的 CLI 框架和 TUI 库来提供漂亮的文档。

应该可以使用 CLI 工具来：

  • 查找有关动词和参数的所有信息（请参阅有关示例的下一点）
  • 获取有关工具本身及其操作原理的信息
  • 发现该工具提供的功能（请参阅有关构建语法的部分）

虽然我从未这样做过，但有些工具甚至提供了完整的教程作为其功能集的一部分！

考虑使用漂亮的库直接在命令行上呈现 markdown，例如glamor (golang) 或rich (python)。

将示例添加到您的文档中

帮助人们开始使用您的工具的最简单方法是提供大量示例。 这通常可以更直观地描述参数以及它们如何相互作用。 用它作为展示某些模式的机会，并提供一些关于为什么某个动词存在的背景信息。 以此为契机，展示使用 CLI 的复杂和高级方法，尤其是在您提供过滤和模板功能的情况下（见下文）。

让它漂亮

谁不喜欢漂亮多彩的 CLI 工具。 那里有很多终端 TUI 和样式管理库。 但是，不要忘记使您的工具与无色终端和 IO 管道兼容。

我在所有的charmbracelet库上玩得很开心，比如用于构建TUI 的bubbletea和用于造型的lipbgloss 。 使其具有交互性

对于某些应用程序（不是全部），我发现实现 CLI 工具的交互式版本很有用。 通常，我在控制硬件时会这样做，其中保持上下文很重要，并且在长时间运行的应用程序中更容易。

这可以像使用rlwrap包装一个简单的 stdin/stdout 循环一样简单，一直到使用功能齐全的 TUI 库，如bubbletea (golang)、 textual (python) 或imtui (c++)。

使用 asciinema 录制短教程

命令行应用程序的一大特点是记录终端会话非常轻量级，可以轻松转换为 gif 或呈现为网页。

像asciinema这样的工具非常适合展示现实生活中的工作流程。

精心制作 CLI 语法

这是我在构建 CLI 工具时一直发现的最困难的事情：设计一个好的语法。 在最好的情况下，动词和子命令是“直观的”：如果用户想要做某事，他们应该很容易猜出使用哪个命令。

这意味着如果您有一个get命令，则有一个set命令，或者对于每个可以操作的资源（例如files 、 hosts 、 drives ）都有一个适当的子命令。

一个令人困惑的语法是使用get --update来设置一个变量，或者使用动词ls-files来列出文件，使用hosts list子命令和动词来列出主机。

我发现我经常需要重写 CLI 动词结构几次，然后才能理解什么对我想要解决的问题有效。 不要试图在第一次就做好，事实上要避免第一次过于“框架化”或过于聪明。 在构建工具时随意添加您认为有用的动词和标志，然后在不介意从头开始重新启动动词和标志的地方进行第二次传递。 您可能会构建可重用的内部辅助函数。

添加结构化输入和输出

为了更轻松地传输和转换工具生成的数据，请考虑将结构化数据与人类可读数据一起输出。

我有生成的通用包装器：

  • 格式良好（和彩色）的表格化、人类可读的输出
  • 不同的预配置人类可读输出选项（例如--wide 、 --pretty 、 --oneline ）
  • 结构化 JSON 输出（这通常是最简单的，因为 CLI 操作的对象通常可以开箱即用地序列化）
  • 适用时，将数据作为 SQLite 数据库转储非常有用

类似地，不仅可以从 CLI，还可以从文件中轻松读取结构化输入。 这使得重新运行类似命令或在团队成员之间共享大批量数据变得更加容易。

有关这些概念的良好示例，请查看kubectl ，用于管理 kubernetes 资源。

添加过滤和聚合选项

结合前面添加结构化 IO 的概念，考虑添加过滤选项。 例如，您可以让用户选择仅将 3 个属性输出为表格数据，或按某个其他属性聚合计数。

作为实现您自己的过滤和聚合的替代方法，这并不总是那么容易，请考虑在您的 CLI 文档中使用jq 、 yq或xsv等工具添加过滤和处理数据的示例。

不要忘记提供大量示例（对现实世界越有用和适用越好），因为作为用户提出这些示例通常很耗时，尤其是当您只需要工具来完成某事时。 把你给出的每一个有用的例子想象成某人将来会体验到的一点快乐。

添加模板选项

如果您想进一步超越结构化输出，请考虑为用户提供提供输出模板的选项。 我总是发现为 CLI 编写模板非常耗时，但一旦我弄明白了它就非常有用。 所以不要吝啬例子！

轻松扩展您的 CLI

轻松添加新动词/扩展 CLI 工具。 这样做的一种常见模式是在使用未知动词XXX时查找名为yourtool-XXX的二进制文件是否存在，并将控制权传递给该二进制文件。 这就是gh （GitHub 客户端）和git的操作方式。

即使是简单的 shell 脚本动词也为用户提供了很好的扩展能力。 为了更大的影响，提供一个像gh这样的简单包装框架，它允许用 go 编写的扩展轻松调用主工具本身。

轻松配置 CLI

您可能希望在 CLI 中提供大量默认值和配置选项（凭据、URL 端点、输出设置）。

通过提供以下内容可以轻松配置这些：

  • 环境变量。 这具有使您的工具易于集成到 CICD 中的额外好处，并通过避免在命令行中列出凭据来提供安全性。 它还允许使用direnv 之类的工具轻松自定义每个目录或项目。
  • 配置文件。 一些命令行选项库提供了在命令行解析器旁边使用配置文件的选项（例如 golang 中的viper ）。 否则，使用 JSON、YAML 或 TOML 解析器读取默认值和覆盖没有什么比这更容易的了。
  • 提供动词来查询和更新您的配置文件。 许多现代 CLI 工具提供动词来与它们自己的配置进行交互。 很好的例子是git config ，它提供了一个丰富的动词来查询、删除、更新、设置 git 配置。 转储和查询当前信息（连同版本、平台、加载的环境和配置文件）可以轻松调试问题。
  • 提供全局和本地配置文件。 除了提供全局配置文件外，还允许用户提供本地配置文件。 使链接配置文件变得容易，这样您就可以拥有一个全局配置，然后是项目范围的配置（检入 git），然后是用户特定的覆盖配置（未检入 git）。

提供自动完成集成

对于用户来说，没有什么比按下 Tab 键并获得一个不错的自动完成更好的了。 如今，Shell 为自动完成提供了非常好的 UI，并提供了一个丰富的框架来利用这些 UI。

除了通过 CLI 参数解析框架使用内置的自动完成生成（见上文），考虑提供动态自动完成选项。 我过去在提供硬件控制工具时使用它来动态查询连接的设备。 自动完成将按 ID 显示已连接小部件的列表。

结论

它们通常很容易编写，对作者自己非常有用，可以作为在开发时练习代码的方法，也是“自我记录”和扩展应用程序库功能的好方法。

稍加思考和设计，CLI 就可以改变游戏规则。

那你呢？ 你会写 CLI 工具吗？ 如果是这样，您使用了哪些技巧？ 您喜欢 TUI 和交互性吗？



原文:

I find command-line tools an incredibly way to multiply the impact of the code they produce.

Did you write a nice library to wrap an API and connect it to your DB model? Then provide a CLI tool that allows anybody to query the API, interact with the DB model, run the operations that you provided, discover all the available functionality and read the API documentation. You can now easily write scripts that exercise different functionality of your code while developing, create pre-commit scripts, create CICD scripts.

Here are a collection of tips I gathered over the years writing CLI tools.

Use a command-line parsing framework

While it is fun to parse argv by hand, I go straight for full-featured command-line parsing frameworks like cobra in go, clap in rust,
click in python, php-cli in PHP.

Not only are these well established, they often allow you to use subcommands, generate autocompletion script for multiple shells, handle pretty-printing of CLI options and link up to configuration frameworks (see below).

Distribute the tool as a single file with no dependencies

To ensure widespread adoption of your CLI tool, it must be easily installable. No one wants to install 80 node modules or god forbid, setup a python virtualenv, just to use your tool.

  • Golang and Rust are perfect languages for this, as they allow you to easily compile static binaries for multiple platforms. You can also experiment with dumping world images in Common Lisp.
  • Another option is to create a docker image that can be run with a single docker call, although this often makes networking and file IO more complicated.
  • A compromise is to make it an easily installable package through your languages package manager (pip, npm, what have you)

Make documentation part of the tool itself

Instead of distributing the documentation as a separate wiki, make it part of your tool. Don't spit out cryptic auto-generated argument lists, but leverage your CLI framework and TUI library to provide beautiful documentation.

It should be possible to use the CLI tool to:

  • lookup all the information about verbs and arguments (see the next point about examples)
  • get information about the tool itself and its principles of operation
  • discover the functionality provided by the tool (see the section about a building a grammar)

While I have never done so, some tools even provide full tutorials as part of their feature set!

As complex the git command line can be, it provides great documentation entries for each verb.

Consider using a pretty library to render markdown straight on the command line, for example glamour (golang) or rich (python).

Add examples to your documentation

The easiest way to help people get started with your tool is to provide numerous examples. This often gives a more intuitive description of arguments and how they interact. Use it as an opportunity to show certain patterns and give some background information about why a certain verb exists. Use it as an opportunity to also show complex and advanced ways of using your CLI, especially if you provide filtering and templating functionality (see below).

git, aws, kubectl all provide examples that I have found extremely useful.

Make it pretty

Who doesn't like a pretty colorful CLI tool. There are a lot of terminal TUI and style management libraries out there. Don't forget to make your tool compatible to colorless terminals and IO pipes however.

I had a great time with all the charmbracelet libraries, like bubbletea for building TUIs and lipbgloss for styling. rich is a similar, awesome python library.

Make it interactive

For some applications (not all), I found implementing an interactive version of the CLI tool useful. Often, I have done so when controlling hardware, where keeping context is important, and much easier in a long running application.

This can be as easy as wrapping a simple stdin/stdout loop with rlwrap, all the way to using full featured TUI libraries like bubbletea (golang), textual (python) or imtui (c++).

Use asciinema to record short tutorials

A great feature of command line applications is that recording a terminal session is very lightweight, and can easily be transformed into a gif or rendered into a webpage.

Tools like asciinema are great to show real-life workflows.

Carefully craft a CLI grammar

This is what I consistently find the most difficult when building a CLI tool: designing a nice grammar. In the best of worlds, verbs and subcommands are "intuitive": if a user wants to do something, it should be easy for them to guess which command to use.

This means having a set command if you have a get command, or a proper subcommand for each resource that can be manipulated (for example files, hosts, drives ).

A confusing grammar would be using get --update to set a variable, or having a verb ls-files for listing files, and a hosts list subcommand and verb for listing hosts.

I find that I often have to rewrite the CLI verb structure a couple of times before I understand what works well for the problem I want to solve. Don't try to get it right the first time, in fact avoiding being too "frameworky" or clever the first time around. Liberally add the verbs and flags you find useful as you are building out your tool, and then do a second pass where you don't mind restarting the verbs and flags from scratch. Chances are that you build internal helper functions that can be reused.

Add structured input and output

CLIs are not just for humans, they are also extremely useful for machines. To make it easier to pipe and convert the data your tools generate, think about outputting structured data alongside human-readable data.

I have generic wrappers that generate:

  • tabulated, human-readable output that is nicely formatted (and colored)
  • different pre-configured human readable output options (for example --wide, --pretty, --oneline)
  • CSV and TSV formats for easy unix tool / spreadsheet consumption
  • structured JSON output (this is usually the easiest, as the objects manipulated by the CLI are often serializable out of the box)
  • when applicable, dumping data out as a SQLite database is extremely useful

Similarly, make it easy to read structured input, not just from the CLI, but also from files. This makes it much easier to rerun similar commands or share big batches of data amongst team members.

For good example of these concepts, look at kubectl , used to managed kubernetes resources.

Add filtering and aggregation options

In conjunction with the previous concept of adding structured IO, consider adding filtering options. For example, you could give a user the option to only output 3 attributes as tabular data, or to aggregate counts by a certain other attribute.

As an alternative to implementing your own filtering and aggregating, which is not always easy, consider adding examples where you filter and process data by using tools like jq, yq or xsv in your CLI documentation.

Don't forget to give copious examples (the more useful and applicable to the real-world, the better), as these are often time-consuming to come up as a user, especially when you just need the tool to get something done. Think of each useful example you give as a little nugget of pleasure that someone will experience in the future.

Add templating options

If you want to go even a step further beyond structured output, consider giving the user the option to provide output templates. Tools like kubectl or aws allow you to pass your own go-template inspired templates, making it possible to create exactly the output needed.

kubectl get no -o go-template='{{range .items}}{{if .spec.unschedulable}}{{.metadata.name}} {{.spec.externalID}}{{"\n"}}{{end}}{{end}}'

I always found writing templates for CLIs quite time-consuming, but extremely useful once I figured it out. So don't skimp on examples!

Make it easy to extend your CLI

Make it easy to add new verbs / extend the CLI tool. A common pattern to do so is to lookup if a binary called yourtool-XXX exists when an unknown verb XXX is used, and pass control to that binary. This is how gh (GitHub client) and git operate.

Even simple shell script verbs provide great extension capability for users. For even bigger impact, provide a simple wrapper framework like gh does, that allows extensions written in go to easily call the main tool itself.

Make it easy to configure your CLI

Chances are that you will want to provide a lot of default values and configuration options in your CLI (credentials, URL endpoints, output settings).

Make it easy to configure these by providing:

  • environment variables. This has the added benefit of making your tool easy to integrate in CICD, and provide security by avoiding listing credentials in your command line. It also allows for easy customization per directory or project, using a tool like direnv.
  • configuration files. A few command line option libraries provide the option to use a configuration file alongside the command line parser (for example viper in golang). Otherwise, nothing is easier to use a JSON, YAML, or TOML parser to read in defaults and overrides.
  • provide verbs to query and update your config file. A lot of modern CLI tools provide verbs to interact with their own configuration. Good examples are git config, which provides a rich verb to query, remove, update, set the git configuration. Dumping and querying the current information (alongside version, platform, loaded environments and config files) makes it easy to debug issues.
  • provide global and local configuration files. Besides providing a global configuration file, allow users to provide local configuration files. Make it easy to chain configuration files, so that you can have a global config, followed by a project-wide config (checked into git), followed by a user specific override config (not checked into git).

Provide autocompletion integration

Nothing is nicer for the user than just pressing tab and getting a nice autocompletion. Shells these days provide really nice UIs for autocompletion, and a rich framework to leverage these.

Besides using the builtin autocompletion generation by CLI argument parsing frameworks (see above), think about providing dynamic autocompletion options. I have used this in the past to dynamically query connected devices when providing a hardware control tool. Autocompletion would show a list of connected widgets by ID.

Conclusion

CLIs and TUIs are incredibly effective tool for the developer. They are usually easy to write, extremely useful for the author themselves as ways to exercise their code while developing, and a great way to "self-document" and extend the functionality of the application our library.

With a bit of thought and styling, CLIs can be turned into game changers.

What about you? Do you write CLI tools? If so, what tricks have you been using? Are you into TUIs and interactivity?

Items.Title: Angular 13 检测屏幕的宽度和高度教程 | Angular 13 Detect Width and Height of Screen Tutorial
Items.Description:
更新 TypeScript 模板
从'@angular/core'包中导入HostListener API，定义变量get screen width和getScreenHeight，使用HostListener绑定windowresize事件来获取window resize时的屏幕尺寸和宽度。

更新 src/app/app.component.ts 文件中的代码。

 import { Component, HostListener } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ selector: 'app-root', templateUrl: './app.component.html', styleUrls: ['./app.component.scss'] }) export class AppComponent { public getScreenWidth: any; public getScreenHeight: any; ngOnInit() { this.getScreenWidth = window.innerWidth; this.getScreenHeight = window.innerHeight; } @HostListener('window:resize', ['$event']) onWindowResize() { this.getScreenWidth = window.innerWidth; this.getScreenHeight = window.innerHeight; } }

更新 HTML 模板
在这一步中，您必须打开 Angular HTML 模板文件并使用双花括号定义变量以在浏览器上打印屏幕或窗口大小。

请更新 src/app/app.component.html 文件中的代码。

 <div class="container text-center mt-5"> <p>Window width: <strong>{{ getScreenWidth }}</strong></p> <p>Window height: <strong>{{ getScreenHeight }}</strong></p> </div>

参考：
https://www.positronx.io/angular-detect-width-and-height-of-screen-tutorial/ https://www.positronx.io/angular-detect-width-and-height-of-screen-tutorial/



原文:

Update TypeScript Template
Import HostListener API from ‘@angular/core’ package, define variables get screen width and getScreenHeight, use the HostListener to bind window to resize event to get the screen size and width on window resize.

Update the code in src/app/app.component.ts file.

import { Component, HostListener } from '@angular/core';

@Component({
  selector: 'app-root',
  templateUrl: './app.component.html',
  styleUrls: ['./app.component.scss']
})
export class AppComponent {

  public getScreenWidth: any;
  public getScreenHeight: any;

  ngOnInit() {
      this.getScreenWidth = window.innerWidth;
      this.getScreenHeight = window.innerHeight;
  }

  @HostListener('window:resize', ['$event'])
  onWindowResize() {
    this.getScreenWidth = window.innerWidth;
    this.getScreenHeight = window.innerHeight;
  }

}

Update HTML Template
In this step, you have to open the angular HTML template file and define the variables using the double curly braces to print the screen or window size on the browser.

Please update the code in src/app/app.component.html file.

<div class="container text-center mt-5">
  <p>Window width: <strong>{{ getScreenWidth }}</strong></p>
  <p>Window height: <strong>{{ getScreenHeight }}</strong></p>
</div>

Reference:
https://www.positronx.io/angular-detect-width-and-height-of-screen-tutorial/
Items.Author: dhanush9952

Items.Title: 停止在节点后端使用 require() | STOP using require() in node backend
Items.Description:
对于所有节点开发人员，您需要停止在新项目中使用 require()。 该节点已经支持模块，本教程将告诉您它们之间的区别以及使用什么来代替 require() 并且还将深入了解模块。

你在用什么

const express = require ( ' express ' ) // common js

你应该使用什么

import express from ' express ' // es module

你有没有看到 ？？ 第二个代码看起来有多好。 第一个是 commonjs 语法，它从源头出现在 node 中，用于导入库，第二个首先在浏览器中引入，然后来到 node.js。

它使代码具有如此多的可读性、现代性和非冗长性。

如何使用它 ？

这简单。

  1. 初始化新节点项目。
  2. 转到您的 package.json。

  3. 添加以下内容。

     "type" : "module" ,

  4. 默认情况下，当您初始化项目时，其设置为 commonjs。

  5. 就是这样，现在开始使用现代 javascript。

常见模式

我将向您展示以模块格式实现的 commonjs 代码，而不是解释它，以便您可以立即开始它，并且将来当您对如何以模块格式执行某些操作感到困惑时，也可以回到本文。

输入

// cjs const express = require ( ' express ' ) // mjs import express from ' express '
 // cjs const express = require ( ' express ' ) const Router = express . Router // mjs import express , { Router } from ' express '
 //cjs const clientRouter = require ( ' express ' ). Router // mjs import { Router as clientRouter } from ' express '

出口

// cjs module . exports = express // mjs export default express
 // cjs module . exports = { router : {...} , utils : {...} } // mjs export { router : {...}, utils : {...} }

一些可能派上用场的更多导出模式

// mjs export default function hello () {...} export const bye = " bye "


原文:

To all the node developer you need to stop using require() in your new project. The node has already support for modules and this tutorial will tell you difference between them and what to use instead of require() and also will deep down into module a bit more.

What are you using

const express = require('express') // common js

What you should use

import express from 'express' // es module

Did you see ?? how much the better code look in second one. The first one is commonjs syntax which is present in node from its origin for importing libraries , the second one was first introduced in browser and then it came to node.

It makes code so much readable , modern and non - verbose.

How to use it ?

Its easy.

  1. Initialise new node project.
  2. Go to your package.json.

  3. Add following to it.

    "type" : "module" ,

  4. By default when you initialise your project its set to commonjs.

  5. That's it now start using modern javascript.

Common patterns

Instead of explaining it I am going to show you commonjs code implemented in module format, so that you can start it immediately , also comeback to this article in future when you are confuse how to do certain things in module format.

Importing 

// cjs
const express = require('express')
// mjs
import express from 'express'

// cjs
const express = require('express')
const Router = express.Router
// mjs
import express , { Router } from 'express'

//cjs
const clientRouter = require('express').Router
// mjs
import { Router as clientRouter } from 'express'

Exporting 

// cjs
module.exports = express
// mjs
export default express

// cjs
module.exports = { router : {...} , utils : {...}
}
// mjs
export { router : {...}, utils : {...}
}

Some more exporting pattern that may come handy 

// mjs
export default function hello() {...}
export const bye = "bye"

Items.Title: 数据结构与算法 | Data Structure & Algorithms
Items.Description:
我目前正在自学和练习数据结构和算法，如果时间允许，我还将学习一些面向对象的编程知识以及系统设计和架构。

正如你已经知道的那样，上面所有的主题都是非常理论和抽象的，而且非常枯燥。 但我和他们的关系又爱又恨。 每次上班看到人们争论不同的软件意见，每当我遇到任何我都会想到的，

实现

经过五年的思考和观察他人和思考，我意识到其中许多主题主要只是概念。 一旦你磨练了它实现的概念，不仅更容易编写，而且更容易解释或传达给其他人的参考。

动机

当用来自给定领域的过去大师数十年的研究和实施支持的信息和知识来解释时，战斗就变得多余了。

可以通过概念和理论的具体证明以及研究支持的证据来恭敬地挑战意见。

如何

我的个人学习

我将使用 JavaScript 作为我的主要精通语言来记录我目前在数据结构和算法方面学习和实践的内容，并且尽可能希望通过使用 JavaScript 来回引用以另一种语言实现相同的知识转移形式.

我还将分享我从 NestJS 学习微服务架构设计所获得的知识。

为了提供更多信息，Node.js 中的 NestJS 就像 Java 世界的 Spring Boot 或 C# 世界的 .NET。

您可以访问他们的网站以了解更多信息。

我的个人分享

将首先从排序算法开始这个系列，因为它们主要用于编码面试，也是练习许多编码概念的好地方。

那么我们还在等什么？ 让我们开始吧。



原文:

I'm currently studying and practising Data Structure and Algorithms on my own and when time permits, I'll also be studying some Object Oriented Programming knowledge alongside Systems Design and Architecturing.

As you can already tell, all of the topics above are all very theoretical and abstract and very very dry. But I have a love-hate relationship with them. Every time I go to work and see people quarrelling about different software opinions and whenever I come across any I would think to myself,

"So what exactly is the better practice?" "Is it true that on some occasions it is good and some not?"

"How do we identify when and what to use then?"

The Realisation

After half a decade of pondering and observing others and thinking I’ve realised that many of these topics are mainly just concepts. Concepts that once you’ve honed it implementation become not only easier to write but also easier to explain or to convey as a reference to others.

The Motivation

FIghts become redundant when explained with information and knowledge backed from decades of research and implementation from past masters of the given field.

Opinions can be respectfully challenged with concrete proof of concepts and theory and research backed evidence.

The How

My Personal Learning

I would be documenting what I am currently learning and practising in Data Structure and Algorithms using JavaScript as my main proficient language and whenever possible I would hopefully be implementing the same in another language as a form of knowledge transfer by making references back and forth with JavaScript.

I would also share knowledge I’ve gained from learning Microservice Architecture designs from NestJS.

To give a little bit more info, NestJS in Node.js is like the Spring Boot of Java world or the .NET of the C# world.

You can visit their website to find out more.

My Personal Sharing

Would be starting this series with Sorting Algorithms first since they are mostly needed in coding interviews and also a great place to practise on many of the coding concepts.

So what are we waiting for? Let’s get started.

Items.Title: 如何 Gzip - 在上传到谷歌云、AWS 时压缩 gcp 文件以获得更好的 SEO | How to Gzip - compress a gcp file while uploading to google cloud , AWS for better SEO
Items.Description:
看一下这个 ！！

现在让我们开始吧

默认情况下，Google Cloud Storage (GCS) 会提供未压缩的文件。 然而，有一个选项可以为选定的文件启用 gzip 压缩。 不过有一个问题。 只能使用静态压缩，这意味着文件需要上传已经压缩到存储中。 一些服务器允许您使用动态压缩，这意味着它们会在客户端请求文件时即时压缩文件，但不幸的是，正如我所提到的，GCS 不会这样做。
将多个文件上传到 gcp 有两种方法，我们将使用gsutil

 gsutil -m cp -r -z css,js,map [LOCAL_DIR] gs://[BUCKET]/[REMOTE_DIR]

为了提高页面速度，更多地使用缓存控制

gsutil -h "Content-Type:text/html" \ -h "Cache-Control:public, max-age=3600" cp -r images \ gs://bucket/images

这将有助于减少图像下载时间并提高页面速度

您可以通过使用查看当前在对象上设置的元数据

gsutil ls -L gs://the_bucket/the_object

检查您的网站上是否启用了 gzip 压缩

并检查（例如，通过使用 Web 浏览器的开发工具）响应是否包含content-encoding: gzip和content-type: application/javascript (for JS) headers，例如：

对于 AWS 云前端

打开 CloudFront 控制台，找到您的分配，然后在“行为”选项中将“自动压缩对象”设置为“是”

现在享受灯塔分数

保存这篇文章以备将来参考



原文:

check thi sout !!

Now lets's get started

Google Cloud Storage (GCS) by default serves files uncompressed. There is however an option to enable gzip compression for selected files. There is a catch though. It’s only possible to use static compression which means that the files need to be uploaded already compressed to the storage. Some servers allow you to use dynamic compression which means that they will compress files on the fly when the files are requested by a client but unfortunately GCS, as I mentioned, doesn't do this.
There are two ways to upload multiple files to gcp , we will use gsutil

gsutil -m cp -r -z css,js,map [LOCAL_DIR] gs://[BUCKET]/[REMOTE_DIR]

-z this z will take care of compressing your files before uploading to gcp and it will serve compressed files to user so that the page speed will be increased

To increase page speed more use cache control also 

gsutil -h "Content-Type:text/html" \
       -h "Cache-Control:public, max-age=3600" cp -r images \
       gs://bucket/images

this will helps reduce the image dowloading time and also increase your page speed

You can see what metadata is currently set on an object by using

gsutil ls -L gs://the_bucket/the_object

check if the gzip compression enabled or not on your website

and checking (for example, by using the web browser's development tools) if the response contains content-encoding: gzip and content-type: application/javascript (for JS) headers, e.g.:

For Aws Cloud front

open up the CloudFront Console, locate your distribution, and set Compress Objects Automatically to Yes in the Behavior options

Now enjoy the light house Score

Save this post for future reference

Items.Title: Kesalahan menggunakan 模式匹配不像任何 di PostgreSQL | Kesalahan menggunakan pattern matching not like any di PostgreSQL
Items.Description:
西哈尔辛博隆

发表于参考
卡兰·普拉塔普·辛格—— Dec 1 '21 21 年 12 月 1 日



原文:

Sihar Simbolon

Posted on May 11

Biasanya saya menggunakan pattern matching menggunakan keyword "not in".

SELECT kolom1 from tabel where kategori not in ('A', 'B')

Kemudian saya mencoba menggunakan keyword "not like any"

SELECT kolom1 from tabel where kategori not like any (array['A', 'B'])

Menurut saya, hasilnya akan sama. Tetapi setelah dijalankan hasilnya berbeda. Saya penasaran kenapa hasil berbeda dan setelah saya cari tahu, cara kerja keyword any itu adalah akan bernilai benar jika salah satu elemen terpenuhi. Jadi jika elemen A dibandingkan ke keyword not like any akan bernilai true karena memenuhi kriteria bukan memenuhi salah satu elemen di dalam array yaitu B. Begitu juga sebaliknya. Sementara hasil yang diinginkan adalah bukan elemen A dan juga bukan elemen B. Untuk menjawab masalah ini digunakan keyword "not like all"

SELECT kolom1 from tabel where kategori not like all (array['A', 'B'])

Referensi
stackoverflow

Karan Pratap Singh - Dec 1 '21

Items.Title: Git：提交 | Git: Commit
Items.Description:
1. 做出改变
  2. 添加更改
  3. 犯罪

在 Git 跟踪的位置创建新文件、编辑文件、删除文件等。

将特定更改组合在一起，以准备提交。 在此步骤中添加的更改尚未提交到 repo 。 将此步骤视为暂存步骤/区域。
git add [file1] [file2]
git add --all

提交之前添加的所有内容。
git commit -m "Describe the commit here"
如果没有此标志，将启动文本编辑器以提供提交消息。

Olubisi Idris Ayinde💡 - Olubisi Idris Ayinde💡 - May 6 5月6日



原文:

  1. Make Changes
  2. Add Changes
  3. Commit

Make new files, edit files, delete files, etc. in a location that is tracked by Git.

2. Add Changes

Group specific changes together, in preparation of committing. The changes added in this step are not committed yet to the repo. Consider this step as a Staging step/area.
git add [file1] [file2]
git add --all

3. Commit

Commit everything that was previously added.
git commit -m "Describe the commit here"
The -m flag is to pass an inline commit message. Without this flag, a text editor will be launched to give a commit message.

Olubisi Idris Ayinde💡 - May 6
Items.Author: jgunawancode

Items.Title: Golang中实用SOLID：接口隔离原理 | Practical SOLID in Golang: Interface Segregation Principle
Items.Description:
阿博拉吉奥耶林德

发表于每当有人开始上第一堂编程课时，程序总是相似的：在最初的几周甚至几个月里，都是关于算法和重新连接大脑以接受这种思维方式。

在某些时候，我们首先介绍面向对象的编程。 如果教师对那个切换等待太多，可能很难切断与函数式编程的联系。

但是，在某些时候，我们接受使用对象。 我们开始在需要的地方使用它们，当然，在不需要的地方。 然后我们开始学习抽象以及如何使我们的代码越来越可重用。

然后，我们可能会开始以错误的方式使用抽象，将它们添加到任何地方。 为了使我们的代码更通用，我们进行了泛化，从而减缓了我们未来的发展。

迟早，我们开始意识到我们应该为如此多的概括划清界限。 幸运的是，有人已经做到了——通过引入接口隔离原则，它代表 SOLID 中的字母 I。

当我们不尊重接口隔离时

保持界面小，这样用户就不会依赖于他们不需要的东西。

保持接口尽可能小的简单声明我们不应该仅仅理解为单一方法接口，而是更多地在接口所具有的特性的内聚性的上下文中查看。

让我们检查下面的代码： `type User interface { AddToShoppingCart(product Product) IsLoggedIn() bool Pay(money Money) error HasPremium() bool HasDiscountFor(product Product) bool // // 一些附加方法 //

让我们假设我们要交付一个购物应用程序。 一种方法是定义一个用户界面，就像我们在代码示例中所做的那样。 该界面包含用户可以拥有的许多功能。

我们平台上的用户可以将产品添加到购物车。 他们可以买。 他们可以获得特定产品的折扣。 唯一的问题是只有特定用户才能完成所有这些操作。

这个接口的实际实现是三个结构。 第一个是 Guest 结构。 应该是未登录我们系统的用户，但至少他们可以将产品添加到购物车。

第二个实现是 NormalCustomer。 它可以做任何客人可以做的事情，还可以购买产品。 第三个实现是PremiumCustomer，它可以使用我们系统的所有功能。

现在，看看所有这三个结构。 只有 PremiumCustomer 需要所有三种方法。 也许我们可以将它们全部分配给 NormalCustomer，但毫无疑问，Guest 几乎不需要超过两个。

如果该结构代表未登录的用户，我们为什么还要考虑实现折扣方法？

在这里，我们甚至可以调用 panic 方法，并带有错误“方法未实现”——这在这段代码中会更诚实。 在通常情况下，我们甚至不应该从 Guest 调用 HasPremium 方法。

我们所做的所有这些都是为了在 UserService 中添加泛化，以在同一个地方使用相同的代码处理所有类型的用户。 但是，正因为如此，我们需要实现一堆未使用的方法。

因此，为了更好地概括，我们得到：

  1. 许多具有未使用方法的结构。
  2. 我们需要以某种方式标记的方法，以便其他人不使用它们。
  3. 用于单元测试的许多附加代码。
  4. 非自然多态性。

所以，让我们重构这个烂摊子。

我们如何尊重接口隔离

围绕最小内聚特性组构建接口。

我们不需要在这里发明一些空间科学。 唯一需要的是定义一个提供一整套功能的最小接口。 让我们检查下面的代码：

现在，我们有三个接口，而不是一个。 此外，他们每个人都介绍了一种方法。

用户现在仅代表尚未在我们平台上进行身份验证的客户。 对于这种类型，我们知道他们可以使用 ShoppingCart 的功能。

新的 LoggedInUser 接口代表我们所有经过身份验证的客户，PremiumUser 接口代表所有具有付费高级帐户的经过身份验证的客户。

请注意：我们确实添加了两个接口，但我们删除了两个方法：IsLoggedIn 和 HasPremium。 这些方法不是我们接口签名的一部分。 但是没有它们我们怎么能工作呢？

正如您在 UserService 中看到的，我们没有使用带有布尔结果的方法，而是澄清了用户界面的子类型。 如果 User 实现了 LoggedInUser，我们就知道我们谈论的是经过身份验证的客户。

此外，如果 User 实现 PremiumUser，我们知道我们谈论的是具有高级帐户的客户。 因此，通过强制转换，我们已经检查了一些业务规则。

除了这两种方法之外，以前的所有结构现在都更加轻量级了。 他们每个人都有五个方法，其中许多根本没有使用，现在他们只有他们真正需要的方法。

概括
它教会我们使我们的界面尽可能小。

每当我们想要覆盖更多类型时，我们应该使用不同的接口来保护它们。 我们应该避免让我们的接口太小，但要提供完整的功能。



原文:

Abolaji Oyerinde

Posted on May 11

Whenever someone starts with first programming lessons, the procedure is always similar: for the first couple of weeks or even months, it is all about algorithms and rewiring the brain to accept that way of thinking.

At some point, we start with an introduction to Object-Oriented Programming. If teachers wait too much for that switch, it may be difficult to cut off the connection to functional programming.

But, at some point, we accept to use objects. And we start using them where is needed, and of course, where is not. Then we start learning about abstractions and how to make our code more and more reusable.

And then, we may start using abstractions in the wrong way, adding them everywhere for everything. To make our code more general, we make generalizations that slow down our future development.

Sooner or later, we start realizing that we should draw a line for so many generalizations. Luckily, somebody already did it — by introducing The Interface Segregation Principle, which stands for letter I in the word SOLID.

When we do not respect The Interface Segregation

Keep interfaces small so that users don’t end up depending on things they don’t need.

Its simple statement to keep interfaces as small as possible we should not understand as just one-method-interface, but to look more in a context of the features’ cohesion that interface holds.

Let us examine the code below: `type User interface { AddToShoppingCart(product Product) IsLoggedIn() bool Pay(money Money) error HasPremium() bool HasDiscountFor(product Product) bool // // some additional methods //

}

type Guest struct { cart ShoppingCart // // some additional fields //

}

func (g *Guest) AddToShoppingCart(product Product) { g.cart.Add(product)

}

func (g *Guest) IsLoggedIn() bool { return false

}

func (g *Guest) Pay(Money) error { return errors.New("user is not logged in")

}

func (g *Guest) HasPremium() bool { return false

}

func (g *Guest) HasDiscountFor(Product) bool { return false

}

type NormalCustomer struct { cart ShoppingCart wallet Wallet // // some additional fields //

}

func (c *NormalCustomer) AddToShoppingCart(product Product) { c.cart.Add(product)

}

func (c *NormalCustomer) IsLoggedIn() bool { return true

}

func (c *NormalCustomer) Pay(money Money) error { return c.wallet.Deduct(money)

}

func (c *NormalCustomer) HasPremium() bool { return false

}

func (c *NormalCustomer) HasDiscountFor(Product) bool { return false

}

type PremiumCustomer struct { cart ShoppingCart wallet Wallet policies []DiscountPolicy // // some additional fields //

}

func (c *PremiumCustomer) AddToShoppingCart(product Product) { c.cart.Add(product)

}

func (c *PremiumCustomer) IsLoggedIn() bool { return true

}

func (c *PremiumCustomer) Pay(money Money) error { return c.wallet.Deduct(money)

}

func (c *PremiumCustomer) HasPremium() bool { return true

}

func (c *PremiumCustomer) HasDiscountFor(product Product) bool { for _, p := range c.policies { if p.IsApplicableFor(c, product) { return true }

}

}

type UserService struct { // // some fields //

}

func (u *UserService) Checkout(ctx context.Context, user User, product Product) error { if !user.IsLoggedIn() { return errors.New("user is not logged in")

}

var money Money
//
// some calculation
//
if user.HasDiscountFor(product) {
    //
    // apply discount
    //
}

return user.Pay(money)

}`

Let us assume we want to deliver an application for shopping. One of the approaches is to define an interface User, as we did in the code example. This interface holds many features a user can have.

A User on our platform can add a Product to the ShoppingCart. They can buy it. They can get a discount on a particular Product. The only problem is that only a specific User can do all of that.

The actual implementations for this interface are three structs. The first one is the Guest struct. It should be a User who is not logged in on our system, but at least they can add a Product to the ShoppingCart.

The second implementation is the NormalCustomer. It can do whatever Guest can, plus to buy a Product. The third implementation is thePremiumCustomer, which can use all features of our system.

Now, look at all of those three structs. Only PremiumCustomer requires all three methods. Maybe we can assign all of them to NormalCustomer, but definitely, we hardly need more than two for Guest.

Methods HasPremium and HasDiscountFor do not have any sense for Guest. If that struct represents the User who is not logged in, why would we even consider implementing methods for discounts?

Here we may even call the panic method, with the error “method is not implemented” — that would be more honest in this code. In the usual scenario, we should not even call the method HasPremium from Guest.

And we did all of this to add generalization inside UserService to handle all types of Users in the same place, with the same code. But, because of that, we need to implement a bunch of unused methods.

So, to have better generalization, we got:

  1. Many structs with unused methods.
  2. Methods that we need somehow to mark so that others do not use them.
  3. Much additional code for unit testing.
  4. Unnatural polymorphism.

So, let us refactor this mess.

How we do respect The Interface Segregation

Build interfaces around the minimal cohesive group of features.

We do not need to invent some space science here. The only needed is to define a minimal interface that delivers a complete set of features. Let us check the code below:

`type User interface { AddToShoppingCart(product Product) // // some additional methods //

}

type LoggedInUser interface { User Pay(money Money) error // // some additional methods //

}

type PremiumUser interface { LoggedInUser HasDiscountFor(product Product) bool // // some additional methods //

}

type Guest struct { cart ShoppingCart // // some additional fields //

}

func (g *Guest) AddToShoppingCart(product Product) { g.cart.Add(product)

}

type NormalCustomer struct { cart ShoppingCart wallet Wallet // // some additional fields //

}

func (c *NormalCustomer) AddToShoppingCart(product Product) { c.cart.Add(product)

}

func (c *NormalCustomer) Pay(money Money) error { return c.wallet.Deduct(money)

}

type PremiumCustomer struct { cart ShoppingCart wallet Wallet policies []DiscountPolicy // // some additional fields //

}

func (c *PremiumCustomer) AddToShoppingCart(product Product) { c.cart.Add(product)

}

func (c *PremiumCustomer) Pay(money Money) error { return c.wallet.Deduct(money)

}

func (c *PremiumCustomer) HasDiscountFor(product Product) bool { for _, p := range c.policies { if p.IsApplicableFor(c, product) { return true }

}

}

type UserService struct { // // some fields //

}

func (u *UserService) Checkout(ctx context.Context, user User, product Product) error { loggedIn, ok := user.(LoggedInUser) if !ok { return errors.New("user is not logged in")

}

var money Money
//
// some calculation
//
if premium, ok := loggedIn.(PremiumUser); ok && premium.HasDiscountFor(product)  {
    //
    // apply discount
    //
}

return loggedIn.Pay(money)

}`

Now, instead of one, we have three interfaces. PremiumUser embeds LoggedInUser, which embeds User. In addition, each of them introduces one method.

The User now represents only customers who are still not authenticated on our platform. For such type, we know they can use features of ShoppingCart.

The new LoggedInUser interface represents all our authenticated customers, and thePremiumUser interface represents all authenticated customers with a paid premium account.

Notice this: we indeed added two more interfaces, but we erased two methods: IsLoggedIn and HasPremium. Those methods are not part of our interface signature. But how can we work without them?

As you see in the UserService, instead of using methods with the boolean result, we just clarify the subtype of the User interface. If User implements LoggedInUser, we know that we talk about the authenticated customer.

Also, if User implements PremiumUser, we know that we talk about the customer with a premium account. So, by casting, we already check for some business rules.

Besides those two methods, all structs from before are now more lightweight. Instead of each of them having five methods, where many of them are not used at all, now they just have methods they really need.

Summary
The Interface Segregation Principle is the fourth SOLID principle, and it stands for the letter I in the word SOLID. It teaches us to make our interfaces as tiny as possible.

Whenever we want to cover more types, we should protect them with different interfaces. We should avoid making our interfaces too small but deliver complete functionality.

Items.Title: 互联网占全球能源的 20% | Is the internet responsible for 20% of global energy
Items.Description:
大家好，这是我的第一篇文章，旨在创建一个话题。 我认为这是一个很好的介绍。 这些是一些非常难以衡量的指标。

在这篇文章中，我想慢慢引导您了解拥有网络存在的有形成本，以及我们都可以做些什么来改善万维网——网络的好处是不可否认的。 没有回头路，但是我们可以减少我们的碳足迹并提供更好的服务和开发人员。

我将从 Youtube 开始——它显然是一个非常繁重的网站，每天有数十亿人在使用它。 让我们深入了解一下灯塔测试。

谷歌曾经有一句话——不作恶。 这不再是他们公司的座右铭。 我的桌面测试表明，它们可以在每个页面的第一次交互中节省大约 1 兆字节。 请自己看一下，这张图片只是他们可以缩小的许多脚本和样式之一。 他们犯了不清除样式和脚本的新手错误。

这并没有阻止训练营用 React 的方式而不是为什么来编程人们。

这是我们遇到第一个瓶颈的地方。 我们有很多框架来制作应用程序，但是一个好的网站是轻量级的，它使用尽可能多的 HTML 和 css。 理想情况下不需要编写脚本。 客户端水合似乎几乎是可笑的 - 使用繁重的脚本语言来说每次访问它时从头开始构建这个站点以允许我们的虚拟/大量修改的 DOM 工作。

随着您变得更高级，您将了解打包和缓存。 现在这就是事情开始变得危险的地方——Gulp 不再是一个任务管理器，看着我们的肩膀，清理和放大，然后吐出预建的站点，准备好以大幅缩小的大小进行 FTP 上传。

不可否认，Webpack 是一个友好的工具，它可能很难使用，而且无论你是否真的需要打包，这也是一个规模问题。 然后是 vite 等人。 这是另一篇文章，如果您对此感兴趣，请告诉我。

我是一名 SEO，我想说这意味着可访问性和性能之间的回报。 使用正确的硬件，如果（网站授予他们访问权限），严重残疾人可以访问网站。 我决定粗略计算一下网络的大小。 这将使活动服务器和云上平均有 40 亿 KB。 它变得更糟。

对我来说，最糟糕的罪魁祸首是“每天有 320 亿人在 Facebook 上活跃”——这是世界实际人口的四倍多。 对于拥有这样流量的企业来说，创建响应式内容是有意义的。 这对最终用户有好处，但请考虑 - 您是在制作应用程序还是在制作网站？

想要真正的踢球者吗？ 已经开始为 Web 开发人员而不是最终用户编写框架和库。 框架和库的战争正在进行，这意味着使用方便是最重要的。 您使用什么形式的渲染？ 在您的站点加载之前，请求从服务器来回 ping 多少次？ 有 42 亿个站点，活跃服务器数量翻了一番。

真正的结论是一根绳子有多长，什么是互联网，我们是否计算了我们所有的设备，我们如何测量以及它是否重要。

更重要的是您的访客使用他们的 3g 手机。 您希望他们获得内容。 互动的时间才是真正重要的。 如果您拥有的每个页面都是 2mb，那么这会花费您的用户，而不仅仅是体验。 您正在慢慢地污染世界并以数据的形式花钱。

那么我为什么要发布这篇文章 - 显然，互联网已经超过了标准媒体，而这个页面的碳成本远低于一张纸。 我小心地只使用一张图片，我使用缓存中的图片来鼓励视图并希望改变视图。

这些统计数据对于思考、可访问性和性能很重要。 让人们访问您的网站，并为他们提供尽可能好的体验。 不要偷懒，从里到外学习你的堆栈，这样你就知道如果你正在泄露数据，你可以破解什么。

请对您的感受发表一些评论 - 显然标题是点击诱饵，但这也是事实。 如果某事使用了无法计算的能量，那么所有优化都是很好的优化。

问候戴夫

优化-U



原文:

Hi All, this is my first post and it is designed to create a talking point. I figured that is as good an intro as any. These are some very hard metrics to measure.

During this post I want to slowly lead you to the tangible cost of having a web presence, and what we can all do to improve the world wide web - the benefits of the web are undeniable. There is no going back, however we can reduce our carbon footprint and provide better services and devs.

I will start with Youtube - it is obviously a very heavy website, used by billions of people every day. Lets take a deep dive with a lighthouse test.

Google used to have a saying - don't be evil. This is no longer their company motto. My desktop testing shows that they could save around a megabyte on every single page's first interaction. Please take a look for yourself, this image is just one of many scripts and styles they could minify. They make the rookie error of not purging their styles and scripts.

Javascript injection even when used for good is a dangerous toy which feels very much like a resurrection of flash - "blazingly fast" but not designed to make sites or be crawled by search engine bots. That doesn't stop bootcamps programming people with the how but not the why of React.

Here is where we hit the first bottleneck. People on 3g networks is really where the tangible cost of our gluttony comes in and worse programmers often ignore accessibility.

We have a so many frameworks to make apps, however a good website is lightweight, it uses as much HTML and css as possible. Ideally no scripting is required. It seems almost laughable having client side hydration - to use a heavy scripting language to say build this site from scratch each time you visit it to allow our virtual/heavily modified DOM to work.

As you get more advanced you learn about packaging and caching. Now this is where things start to get dangerous - Gulp is no longer a task manager looking over our shoulders, purging and magnifying, then spitting out prebuilt sites ready for FTP upload at a massive reduction in size.

Webpack blew it out the water. Webpack is undeniably a friendly tool, it can be hard to use, and again it is a case of scale whether you actually need packaging or not. Then there is vite et al. That is another post, please let me know if this interests you.

I am an SEO, and I like to say that means the payoff between accessibility and performance. With the right hardware severely disabled people can access sites if (the sites grant them access). SEO is not about linkspam, it is about delivering the best content you can, as quickly as you can to as many people as you can.

I decided to roughly calculate the size of the web. The average desktop page is 2mb, with many sites significantly higher than that. "There are around two billion websites [in 2022]".

That would make an average of 4bn kilobytes sat on active servers and on the cloud. It gets worse.

1.4.4bn of our YouTube videos are watched.

For me the worst culprit is that "32 billion people are active on Facebook daily" - that's more than four times the actual population of the world. Facebook maintains React. It makes sense for a business with traffic like that to create reactive content. It is good for the end user, however consider - are you making an app or are you making a site?

Want the real kicker? :These stats are from 2017.

Frameworks and libraries have started to be written for the web dev rather than the end user. There is a war of frameworks and libraries going on which means convenience of use is paramount. React is winning because it has the highest user-base, not because it is the fastest, lightest weight, or best by any other metric.

What form of rendering do you use? How many times do requests ping back and forth from the server before your site loads? There are 4.2bn sites and double that amount of active servers.

[In 2017 the guardian predicted that the internet would account for 20% of the worlds electricity in 2025.]

The real conclusion is how long is a piece of string, what is the internet, do we count all our devices how do we measure and does it matter.

What is more important is that your visitors with their 3g phones. You want them to get content. Time to interaction is what is really important. If each page you have is 2mb that is costing your user, not just in experience. You are slowly polluting the world and costing money in the form of data.

So why am I making this post - obviously there was a point where the internet overtook standard media, and this page costs far less carbon than a piece of paper. I am careful only to use one image, and I am using that from a cache to encourage views and hopefully change views.

These stats are important to think about, accessibility and performance. Get people on your site and give them the best experience possible. Don't get lazy, learn your stack inside out so you know what you can hack away if you are bleeding data.

Please leave some comments on what you feel - obviously the title is clickbait but it is also the truth. If something uses an incalculable amount of energy, all optimisation is good optimsation.

Regards Dave

Optimise-U

Items.Title: 创建你的第一个 npm 包！ | Create your first npm package!
Items.Description:
什么是 npm？

来自各大洲的开源开发人员使用 npm 来共享和借用包。 您可以创建自己的 npm 包供个人使用，同时其他开发人员也可以使用它！

又怎样？

  1. 创建要在 npm 中发布的代码，它可以是 javascript 函数或反应组件。 您认为对您和他人有用的任何东西。

例子：

此函数将生成带有字母的随机数，可用作 ID

const generateID = () => { const alphabetArray = " abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXWZ " . split ( "" ); const numberArray = " 1234567890 " . split ( "" ); let ID = []; for ( let i in alphabetArray ) { const random = Math . floor ( Math . random () * i + 1 ); if ( ID . length <= 5 && numberArray [ i ]) { ID . push ( numberArray [ random ] + alphabetArray [ random ]); } } return `0x- ${ ID . join ( " - " )} ` ; }; export default generateID ;

确保文件的名称是 index.js 或者它可以是任何东西，只需编辑你的 package.json

如您所见，代码是使用 module.exports 导出的，这一步对于您在其他地方使用您的包非常重要。

  1. 为你的 npm 包创建一个 github 存储库。 我们创建 github 存储库的原因是 package.json 文件将所有 github 信息链接到其中。

确保用你自己的 repo 更改“repo-link”

 echo "# repo-link" >> README.md git init git add README.md git commit -m "first commit" git branch -M main git remote add repo-link git push -u origin main
  1. 现在，初始化 npm，你的终端会询问你包的信息，但我们可以提前初始化它。 只需在第一个问题上输入您的包裹名称，其余的都是可选的。

小鲁本·沃克 - May 10 5月10日



原文:

What is npm?

npm is the world's largest software registry. Open source developers from every continent use npm to share and borrow packages. you can create your own npm package for personal use, at the same time other developers can use it!

SO... HOW?

  1. Create the code that you want to publish in npm, it could be a javascript function or a react component. anything that you think can be useful for you and to others.

example:

this function is going to generate random number with letters in it that can be use as ID

const generateID = () => { const alphabetArray =
"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXWZ".split(""); const numberArray = "1234567890".split(""); let ID = []; for (let i in alphabetArray) { const random = Math.floor(Math.random() * i + 1); if (ID.length <= 5 && numberArray[i]) { ID.push(numberArray[random] + alphabetArray[random]); } } return `0x-${ID.join("-")}`;
}; export default generateID;

make sure the name of the file is index.js or it could be anything just edit your package.json

as you can see the code is exported using module.exports, this step is really important for you to use your package in other place.

  1. Create a github repo for your npm package. the reason why we are creating a github repo is for the package.json file to have all of the github information linked inside of it.

make sure to change the "repo-link" with your own repo

echo "# repo-link" >> README.md
git init
git add README.md
git commit -m "first commit"
git branch -M main
git remote add repo-link
git push -u origin main
  1. Now, initialize the npm, your terminal will ask you about the information of your package, but we are able to initialize it earlier. Just enter the name of your package on the first question and the rest is optional.

Reuben Walker, Jr. - May 10

Items.Title: 项目规划在定制软件开发中的重要性 | Importance of Project Planning in Custom Software Development
Items.Description:
介绍
当一家公司决定开始一个项目时，开发人员做的最重要的事情就是制定一个项目计划。 听起来很容易，对吧？ 但事实并非如此！

如果没有适当的项目计划，定制软件开发项目可能会面临无法满足其所有可交付成果甚至失败的风险。

因此，在本博客中，我们将告诉您为什么项目规划在定制软件开发中如此重要——不仅对项目，而且对整个公司都是如此。

什么是软件开发项目计划？
软件开发项目计划是指成功完成项目所必需的活动和资源的有序和有组织的过程。 开发项目包含的活动由团队必须遵守的时间表定义。 该计划详细描述了软件开发项目的所有阶段。 一家软件开发公司根据客户的要求制定产品开发草案。

必须预先定义项目的要求、概念和交付时间表。 因此，项目的质量很大程度上取决于软件开发项目计划的构建程度。

软件开发项目计划的主要阶段是什么？ 无论项目规模大小，整个软件开发项目计划主要分为四个阶段：

软件开发项目规划的阶段——深入挖掘

在本文的这一部分，我们将详细讨论软件开发项目规划的 4 个重要阶段。 但在此之前，重要的是要了解，除了有效的计划之外，软件开发项目的需求是对项目经理的需求。

项目经理负责整个项目，从计划到实施和执行。 此外，项目经理具有以下每个软件开发项目规划阶段所必需的所有先决条件。 大多数软件开发服务都是通过满足以下提到的 4 个重要阶段而设计的：

一、范围
项目范围是每个项目最重要的方面之一。 项目经理必须批准您提出的软件开发项目计划。 我们正在强调现阶段项目的所有关键点。

一些重要的讨论点包括：

项目描述 合规矩阵 讨论假设 定义目标和结果 讨论限制

软件开发项目规划的基础是理解和定义一个精确的范围。 有必要对你即将发展的想法有一个清晰的想法和理解。

要求包括以下所有内容的列表：

主要执行任务 功能 架构 设计 沟通计划

逻辑

如果您想要项目总成本的近似数字，则需要将整个项目划分为子组件。 一旦分成子组件，您会发现确定每个子组件的成本比确定整个项目的预算更容易。 您还可以为这些子组件中的每一个提出单独的时间表。

详细的组件和设备清单将帮助软件开发团队对总成本进行粗略估计。 您将对项目的总期限及其总成本有一个粗略的了解。

一旦您完成了需求列表，软件开发团队就可以估计将您的产品转变为功能齐全的市场适合产品所需的时间。

思考要点
任何软件开发项目规划的两个关键因素是总成本以及构建产品的总时间。 但是，构建任何软件项目所需的时间取决于项目的要求和可用资源。

当您谈到移动应用程序开发时，如果有合适的开发人员，您将需要大约三个月的时间将您的想法转化为功能齐全的应用程序。 此外，与大型软件开发项目相比，移动应用程序开发涉及较少的复杂性。

其次，有效的软件开发项目计划对于以下方面至关重要：

产品所有者和共同所有者 利益相关者 产品开发团队 软件开发团队 项目经理

最后，有效的软件开发项目计划是获得成本效益、可靠和高效的软件解决方案的清晰方法。 此外，定制软件开发公司易于开发，对客户来说简单易用。

结论：如何确保您的软件开发项目计划有效？

如果您聘请开发人员来满足您的软件开发需求，Narola Infotech 可以为您提供帮助。 我们是美国和印度领先的定制软件开发服务提供商。

来源： https ://www.narolainfotech.com/blogs/project-planning-custom-software-development/



原文:

Introduction
When a company decides to start a project, the foremost thing a developer does is create a project plan. Sounds easy, right? But it’s not!

Without a proper project plan, a custom software development project may be at risk of not meeting all its deliverables or even failing.

Thus, in this blog, we will tell you why project planning is so important in custom software development— not just for the project but also for the entire company.

What is Software Development Project Planning?
A software development project planning refers to a sequential and organized process of activities and resources essential to successfully completing a project. The activities that a development project encompasses are defined by timelines that the team must adhere to. The plan describes all the phases of software development projects in detail. A software development company lays out a draft of product development based on the client’s requirements.

The requirements of the project, the concepts, and the timeline of delivery must be pre-defined. Hence, the quality of projects highly depends on how well the software development project plan is built.

What are the Main Stages of Software Development Project Planning? Irrespective of the size of the project, the entire software development project plan is majorly divided into four phases:

Stages of Software Development Project Planning – Digging Deeper

In this section of the article, we will discuss in detail the 4 important stages of software development project planning. But before that, it is important to understand that other than effective planning, the requirement of a software development project is the need for a project manager.

A project manager is responsible for the entire project, from planning to implementation and execution. Besides, the project manager has all the prerequisites essential for each of the below-mentioned stages of software development project planning. Most of the software development services are designed by catering to the 4 important stages mentioned below:

1. Scope
The scope of the project is one of the most important aspects of every project. A project manager must approve the software development project plan that you come up with. We are highlighting all key points of the projects at this stage.

Some important points of discussion include:

Project description Compliance matrix Discussion of assumptions Defining goals and results Discussing the limitations

The foundation of software development project planning is understanding and defining a precise scope. It is necessary to have a clear idea and understanding of the idea that you are about to develop.

2. Requirements
The requirements include a list of all the following:

Major tasks for execution The functions The architecture The designs The communication plan

The logic

3. Detailing the Budget
If you want an approximate figure of the total cost of your project, you will need to divide the entire project into sub-components. Once divided into sub-components, you will find it easier to determine the cost of each of these sub-components rather than deciding the budget of the whole project. You can also come up with individual timescales for each of these sub-components.

An elaborate list of components and equipment will help the software development team land on a rough estimate of the total cost. You will have a rough idea of the total tenure of the project and its total cost.

4. Setting up a Timeline
Once you have finalized the list of requirements, a software development team can estimate the time it would take to turn your product into a fully functional market-suited product.

Points to Ponder
Two crucial factors for any software development project planning are the total cost as well as the total time to build the product. However, the time required to build any software project depends on the project’s requirements and available resources.

When you talk of mobile application development, with the right set of developers, you will require around three months to convert your idea into a fully functional app. Furthermore, Mobile app Development involves lesser complications as compared to a large-scale software development project.

Secondly, an effective software development project plan is essential for:

The product owners and co-owners The stakeholders The product development team The software development team The project managers

Lastly, an effective software development project plan is a crystal clear approach to a cost-efficient, reliable and efficient software solution. Moreover, It is easy for the custom software development company to develop and easy and user-friendly for the customer.

Conclusion: How to Ensure Your Software Development Project Plan is Effective?

If you hire developers to cater to your software development needs, Narola Infotech can come to your rescue. We are a leading Custom Software Development services provider in the US and India.

Items.Title: 建立仓库和商店管理系统 - Pt。 4 - Final 4 - 决赛 | Build Warehouse and Store Management System - Pt. 4 - Final
Items.Description:
那我怎样才能让它真正发挥作用呢？ 好的，我将详细描述（bismillah）。 第一步是准备能够使用上述流程的库。

  • 还有一杯☕

安装 ESCPOS PHP

 composer require mike42/escpos-php

该库为热敏收据打印机实现了爱普生 ESC/POS 协议的子集。 它允许您在兼容的打印机上创建和打印基本、剪切和条形码格式的收据。

安装工人

composer require workerman/workerman

该库用于创建 websocket 服务器，稍后将在浏览器和客户端计算机之间提供桥梁。 以便网站应用程序可以触发打印机。

创建 Websocket 服务器

通过使用workerman ，我们可以很容易地创建一个websocket服务器，这里是创建websocket的代码

<?php use Workerman\Worker ; require_once __DIR__ . '/vendor/autoload.php' ; // Create a Websocket server $ws_worker = new Worker ( 'websocket://0.0.0.0:8889' ); // Emitted when new connection come $ws_worker -> onConnect = function ( $connection ) { echo "New connection open" . PHP_EOL ; }; // Emitted when data received $ws_worker -> onMessage = function ( $connection , $message ) { // Send hallo $data $connection -> send ( 'Hallo ' . $message ); }; // Emitted when connection closed $ws_worker -> onClose = function ( $connection ) { echo "Connection closed" . PHP_EOL ; }; // Run worker Worker :: runAll ();

上面的代码是从 github 上的workerman/workerman页面上的使用示例创建websocket 服务器的示例。

从websocket 服务器示例出发，我们可以轻松地创建一个打印机服务器来与浏览器中的在线网站应用程序进行通信。

当然有一些调整是必须要做的，即我们在使用打印机时的需求：

  • 打印机用于打印收据
  • 显示从数据库到收据的采购数据
  • 打印收据时向浏览器发送信号
  • 在打印机服务器和网站应用程序之间发送连接指示信号

使用 Websocket 创建打印机服务器

<?php use Workerman\Worker ; require_once __DIR__ . '/vendor/autoload.php' ; // Functions function sendToAll ( $connection , $message ) { $connection -> send ( $message ); } function createMessage ( $type = 'info' , $message ) { $timestamps = date ( 'Y/m/d H:i:s' ); $log = strtoupper ( $type ); return "[ $timestamps ][ $log ] $message " ; } // End Functions echo createMessage ( "info" , "Waiting connection..." ) . PHP_EOL ; // Create a Websocket server $ws_worker = new Worker ( 'websocket://0.0.0.0:8182' ); // Emitted when new connection come $ws_worker -> onConnect = function ( $connection ) { $message = createMessage ( "info" , "New connection open" ) . PHP_EOL ; echo $message ; sendToAll ( $connection , $message ); }; // Emitted when data received $ws_worker -> onMessage = function ( $connection , $message ) { // Send hello $data $connection -> send ( 'Hello ' . $message ); }; // Emitted when connection closed $ws_worker -> onClose = function ( $connection ) { echo "Connection closed" . PHP_EOL ; }; // Run worker Worker :: runAll ();

使用名称workerman-printer.php保存它并在终端中使用以下命令运行它：

 php workerman - printer . php start

终端控制台视图

请使用Simple WebSocket Client测试 websocket 连接。

  • 将服务器位置连接到地址ws://127.0.0.1:PORT
  • 尝试使用 Workerman 消息向 WebSocket 服务器发送数据。
  • 在消息日志中查看结果

显示Simple WebSocket Client by Fenjin Wang

为打印机创建 WebSocket 服务器进展顺利，现在可以从浏览器上的在线应用程序打印销售说明格式。

打印时制作收据格式

我将举例说明ESCPOS-PHP中的收据格式。 首先，我们需要创建一个 Wrapper 类来将商品名称和价格设置为列：

 <?php /* A wrapper to do organise item names & prices into columns */ class item { private $name ; private $price ; private $dollarSign ; public function __construct ( $name = '' , $price = '' , $dollarSign = false ) { $this -> name = $name ; $this -> price = $price ; $this -> dollarSign = $dollarSign ; } public function __toString () { $rightCols = 10 ; $leftCols = 38 ; if ( $this -> dollarSign ) { $leftCols = $leftCols / 2 - $rightCols / 2 ; } $left = str_pad ( $this -> name , $leftCols ) ; $sign = ( $this -> dollarSign ? '$ ' : '' ); $right = str_pad ( $sign . $this -> price , $rightCols , ' ' , STR_PAD_LEFT ); return " $left$right 
 " ; } }

然后我们创建一个函数来包装命令ESCPOS-PHP

 <?php function print_receipt ( $arrData ) { switch ( strtolower ( php_uname ( 's' ))) { case "linux" : // Make sure if using /dev/usb/lp0 you have access permissions $connector = new Mike42\Escpos\PrintConnectors\FilePrintConnector ( "/dev/usb/lp0" ); break ; default : // Dynamic printer name $connector = new Mike42\Escpos\PrintConnectors\WindowsPrintConnector ( $arrData [ 'printer_name' ]); break ; } $printer = new Mike42\Escpos\Printer ( $connector ); /* Name of shop */ $printer -> selectPrintMode ( Printer :: MODE_DOUBLE_WIDTH ); $printer -> text ( "ExampleMart Ltd. 
 " ); $printer -> selectPrintMode (); $printer -> text ( "Shop No. 42. 
 " ); $printer -> feed (); /* Title of receipt */ $printer -> setEmphasis ( true ); $printer -> text ( "SALES INVOICE 
 " ); $printer -> setEmphasis ( false ); /* Items */ $printer -> setJustification ( Printer :: JUSTIFY_LEFT ); $printer -> setEmphasis ( true ); $printer -> text ( new item ( '' , '$' )); $printer -> setEmphasis ( false ); $subtotal = []; foreach ( $arrData [ 'items' ] as $item ) { $printer -> text ( new Item ( $item [ 'name' ], $item [ 'price' ])); array_push ( $subtotal , $item [ 'price' ]); } $printer -> setEmphasis ( true ); $printer -> text ( new Item ( 'Subtotal' , array_sum ( $subtotal ))); $printer -> setEmphasis ( false ); $printer -> feed (); /* Tax and total */ $total = array_sum ( $subtotal ) + $arrData [ 'tax' ]; $printer -> text ( new Item ( 'A Local Tax' , $arrData [ 'tax' ])); $printer -> selectPrintMode ( Printer :: MODE_DOUBLE_WIDTH ); $printer -> text ( new Item ( 'Total' , $total )); $printer -> selectPrintMode (); /* Footer */ $date = date ( 'l jS \of FY h:i:s A' ); $printer -> feed ( 2 ); $printer -> setJustification ( Printer :: JUSTIFY_CENTER ); $printer -> text ( "Thank you for shopping at ExampleMart 
 " ); $printer -> text ( "For trading hours, please visit example.com 
 " ); $printer -> feed ( 2 ); $printer -> text ( $date . " 
 " ); /* Cut the receipt and open the cash drawer */ $printer -> cut (); $printer -> pulse (); $printer -> close (); }

打印收据格式就足够了，如果在workerman-printer.php文件中合二为一，我们可以看到代码。 像这样：

 <?php use Workerman\Worker ; use Mike42\Escpos\Printer ; use Mike42\Escpos\PrintConnectors\FilePrintConnector ; use Mike42\Escpos\PrintConnectors\WindowsPrintConnector ; require_once __DIR__ . '/vendor/autoload.php' ; // Functions function sendToAll ( $connection , $message ) { $connection -> send ( $message ); } function createMessage ( $type = 'info' , $message ) { $timestamps = date ( 'Y/m/d H:i:s' ); $log = strtoupper ( $type ); return "[ $timestamps ][ $log ] $message " ; } function print_receipt ( $arrData ) { switch ( strtolower ( php_uname ( 's' ))) { case "linux" : // Make sure if using /dev/usb/lp0 you have access permissions $connector = new FilePrintConnector ( "/dev/usb/lp0" ); break ; default : // Dynamic printer name $connector = new WindowsPrintConnector ( $arrData [ 'printer_name' ]); break ; } $printer = new Printer ( $connector ); /* Name of shop */ $printer -> selectPrintMode ( Printer :: MODE_DOUBLE_WIDTH ); $printer -> text ( "ExampleMart Ltd. 
 " ); $printer -> selectPrintMode (); $printer -> text ( "Shop No. 42. 
 " ); $printer -> feed (); /* Title of receipt */ $printer -> setEmphasis ( true ); $printer -> text ( "SALES INVOICE 
 " ); $printer -> setEmphasis ( false ); /* Items */ $printer -> setJustification ( Printer :: JUSTIFY_LEFT ); $printer -> setEmphasis ( true ); $printer -> text ( new item ( '' , '$' )); $printer -> setEmphasis ( false ); $subtotal = []; foreach ( $arrData [ 'items' ] as $item ) { $printer -> text ( new Item ( $item [ 'name' ], $item [ 'price' ])); array_push ( $subtotal , $item [ 'price' ]); } $printer -> setEmphasis ( true ); $printer -> text ( new Item ( 'Subtotal' , array_sum ( $subtotal ))); $printer -> setEmphasis ( false ); $printer -> feed (); /* Tax and total */ $total = array_sum ( $subtotal ) + $arrData [ 'tax' ]; $printer -> text ( new Item ( 'A Local Tax' , $arrData [ 'tax' ])); $printer -> selectPrintMode ( Printer :: MODE_DOUBLE_WIDTH ); $printer -> text ( new Item ( 'Total' , $total )); $printer -> selectPrintMode (); /* Footer */ $date = date ( 'l jS \of FY h:i:s A' ); $printer -> feed ( 2 ); $printer -> setJustification ( Printer :: JUSTIFY_CENTER ); $printer -> text ( "Thank you for shopping at ExampleMart 
 " ); $printer -> text ( "For trading hours, please visit example.com 
 " ); $printer -> feed ( 2 ); $printer -> text ( $date . " 
 " ); /* Cut the receipt and open the cash drawer */ $printer -> cut (); $printer -> pulse (); $printer -> close (); } // End Functions /* A wrapper to do organise item names & prices into columns */ class item { private $name ; private $price ; private $dollarSign ; public function __construct ( $name = '' , $price = '' , $dollarSign = false ) { $this -> name = $name ; $this -> price = $price ; $this -> dollarSign = $dollarSign ; } public function __toString () { $rightCols = 10 ; $leftCols = 38 ; if ( $this -> dollarSign ) { $leftCols = $leftCols / 2 - $rightCols / 2 ; } $left = str_pad ( $this -> name , $leftCols ) ; $sign = ( $this -> dollarSign ? '$ ' : '' ); $right = str_pad ( $sign . $this -> price , $rightCols , ' ' , STR_PAD_LEFT ); return " $left$right 
 " ; } } echo createMessage ( "info" , "Waiting connection..." ) . PHP_EOL ; // Create a Websocket server $ws_worker = new Worker ( 'websocket://0.0.0.0:8182' ); // Emitted when new connection come $ws_worker -> onConnect = function ( $connection ) { $message = createMessage ( "info" , "New connection open" ) . PHP_EOL ; echo $message ; sendToAll ( $connection , $message ); }; // Emitted when data received $ws_worker -> onMessage = function ( $connection , $message ) { $data = json_decode ( $message , true ); echo '> printing from: ' . $data [ 'from' ] . PHP_EOL ; // to console sendToAll ( $connection , createMessage ( 'info' , 'printing_from: ' . $data [ 'from' ]) ); // to browser echo '> activer_printer: ' . $data [ 'printer_name' ] . PHP_EOL ; // to console sendToAll ( $connection , createMessage ( 'info' , 'active_printer: ' . $data [ 'printer_name' ]) ); // to browser echo '< echo' , " 
 " ; // to console try { print_receipt ( $data ); echo "[INFO][" . date ( 'Ymd h:i:s' ) . "] Staff " . $data [ 'staff_fullname' ] . " print transaction number " . $data [ 'trx_number' ] . PHP_EOL ; // to console sendToAll ( $connection , createMessage ( 'info' , "notifiy: Kasir " . $data [ 'staff_fullname' ] . " print transaction number " . $data [ 'trx_number' ]) ); // to browser } catch ( Exception $e ) { echo "Couldn't print to this printer: " . $e -> getMessage () . " 
 " ; // to console sendToAll ( $connection , createMessage ( 'error' , "Couldn't print to this printer: " . $e -> getMessage ()) ); // to browser } }; // Emitted when connection closed $ws_worker -> onClose = function ( $connection ) { echo "Connection closed" . PHP_EOL ; // to console }; // Run worker Worker :: runAll ();

使用workerman-printer.php文件，您可以尝试使用上述工具对其进行测试。 The format of the data sent from the WebSocket Client to the Server is as follows: WebSocket Client 发送给 Server 的数据格式如下：

 { "trx_number" : "TRX-00017845" , "items" : [ { "name" : "An Example Item #1" , "price" : 1.45 }, { "name" : "Another Item" , "price" : 3.45 }, { "name" : "Something else" , "price" : 2.45 }, { "name" : "Final Item" , "price" : 1.50 } ], "tax" : 1.30 , "from" : "Application Name" , "printer_name" : "ESPON TM-U220" , "staff_fullname" : "Ryan Oz"
}

不要忘记！ 使用以下命令在终端中运行workerman-printer.php文件：

 # run with DEBUG php workerman-printer.php start # or run in background php workerman-printer.php start -d

这是构建仓库和商店管理系统系列的最后一篇文章。 谢谢阅读。
